forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of WA extends a ridge over South Australia. This high moves slowly east to be south of the Bight by tonight. The high is forecast to hover over SA tomorrow, then weaken and step back to the west on Tuesday allowing a series of fronts to pass over the southeast on Wednesday and Thursday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, mostly about the coastal fringe. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures well below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, fresh at times near southern coasts.

Patchy morning fog about the agricultural area. Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, mostly about southern coastal fringes, contracting to southern coasts later. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending easterly in the north. Afternoon sea breezes near the coast.
Morning fog about the agricultural area. Isolated showers about southern coasts. Sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures below average. Mostly light to moderate southeast to southwesterly winds.

text.p: Morning fog about the agricultural area. Showers about the Lower South East, possibly extending to remaining remaining southern coasts. Sunny in the north. Light winds, tending moderate northwesterly about the southeast.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: Up to 2 mm about the southern agricultural area, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the southern coasts and ranges, and also about the northeastern Spencer Gulf region. Negligible falls for northern agricultural area and the south of the Pastoral districts.
